extraction.one_line_summary: GERHEIGH Ruben won solo after following CASTROVIEJO's late attack while NIBALI's test attack failed to produce damage prompting team abandon of effort.
tactical_patterns: BIALOV bridged solo from peloton to 7-man break, immediately counterattacked with 35km showing strength through effort stacking.
NIBALI shark attack prompted immediate look-back assessment and team abort signal when damage insufficient, revealing pre-planned threshold metrics.
CASTROVIEJO unable to shed GERHEIGH despite riding, suggesting stage hunters should expect Portuguese climber in late-race selections.
HAMILTON and KAGAN gaining GC time from final climb selection indicates breakaway positioning critical on medium mountain stages.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: aborted own attack mid-effort after visual assessment showed insufficient gaps, suggesting declining confidence in climbing firepower
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: defended pink jersey through selective climbing on stage with 4500m elevation, now holding GC lead deeper than any Portuguese in history
rider_signals.GERHEIGH Ruben: positive: first grand tour win came from following wheels then finishing stronger, not initiating, revealing patient tactical maturity at 26
rider_signals.CASTROVIEJO Jonathan: negative: failed to drop weaker climber despite leading final climb attack, suggesting diminished late-race power after bridge effort
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: moved closer to pink jersey reaching third overall, gaining time through smart positioning not attacking
